title: AQUA/MODIS Chlorophyll-a Daily L3m Ocean Color — Red Sea
abstract: Daily NASA AQUA/MODIS Level-3 mapped (L3m) ocean color chlorophyll-a concentration granules covering the Red Sea, downloaded per-scene from NASA OceanColor/OB.DAAC and archived internally in three parallel forms: an extracted directory of daily NetCDF granules, a tar bundle of the same granules, and a single time-merged ('combined') NetCDF file spanning the full record. Standard OC3/OCx chlorophyll-a algorithm product (variable 'chlor_a').

spatial.resolution: 4 km (L3m daily, scene extent varies with swath/cloud cover)

resources.name: Daily CHL granules (directory of NetCDF files)

variables.name: chlor_a
variables.label: Chlorophyll-a concentration
variables.description: Near-surface chlorophyll-a concentration from the standard OCx/OC3 empirical band-ratio algorithm applied to AQUA/MODIS L3m daily granules (filename pattern AQUA_MODIS.YYYYMMDD.L3m.DAY.CHL.x_chlor_a.nc; files from 2026 onward carry a .NRT near-real-time flag).

variables.name: chlor_a
variables.label: Chlorophyll-a concentration
variables.description: Same content as the extracted directory resource; tar-packaged copy of daily AQUA/MODIS L3m CHL granules (paths inside archive under requested_files/).

variables.name: chlor_a
variables.label: Chlorophyll-a concentration
variables.description: Apparent time-concatenated merge of the daily CHL granules into one multi-time-step NetCDF file. Internal structure (time dimension length, exact merge method) not verified — file was not opened/parsed.

steward.name: Unassigned - KAUST Red Sea Data Team

access.how_to_request: Contact KAUST Red Sea data team; underlying granules are also freely re-downloadable from NASA OceanColor Web (OB.DAAC) for the same date range and bounding box.

provenance.originator: NASA Goddard Space Flight Center, Ocean Biology Processing Group (OB.DAAC) - AQUA/MODIS
provenance.citation: NASA Ocean Biology Processing Group. AQUA/MODIS Ocean Color Data, Chlorophyll-a Concentration, OCx Algorithm, NASA OB.DAAC.

known_issues: n_rows for the combined NetCDF is inferred from the granule count of the daily-file resources, not verified by opening the file (no NetCDF-reading tool was available in this environment).
File count (7123) does not equal a full daily count over 2005-2026 due to cloud-masked/no-data days already excluded upstream at NASA.
Files after ~2026-01 carry a .NRT (near-real-time) suffix and may later be replaced by reprocessed standard files; duplicates/overlap with final files not checked.
